source_rationale: The immunophenoscore (IPS) is composed of four averaged and weighted Z scores — the EC score that covers expression of effector cells (activated CD4+ T cells, activated CD8+ T cells, effector memory CD4+ T cells, and effector memory CD8+ T cells), the SC score that collects the expression related to immune-suppressive cells (T-regs and Myeloid-derived suppressor cells), the Major Histocompatibility Complex score for MHC-related molecules, and the immune checkpoint score to represent the activity of immune checkpoints or immunomodulators47.

The immunophenoscore (IPS) is composed of four averaged and weighted Z scores — the EC score that covers expression of effector cells (activated CD4+ T cells, activated CD8+ T cells, effector memory CD4+ T cells, and effector memory CD8+ T cells), the SC score that collects the expression related to immune-suppressive cells (T-regs and Myeloid-derived suppressor cells), the Major Histocompatibility Complex score for MHC-related molecules, and the immune checkpoint score to represent the activity of immune checkpoints or immunomodulators47.

The immunophenoscore (IPS) is composed of four averaged and weighted Z scores — the EC score that covers expression of effector cells (activated CD4+ T cells, activated CD8+ T cells, effector memory CD4+ T cells, and effector memory CD8+ T cells), the SC score that collects the expression related to immune-suppressive cells (T-regs and Myeloid-derived suppressor cells), the Major Histocompatibility Complex score for MHC-related molecules, and the immune checkpoint score to represent the activity of immune checkpoints or immunomodulators47.

The immunophenoscore (IPS) is composed of four averaged and weighted Z scores — the EC score that covers expression of effector cells (activated CD4+ T cells, activated CD8+ T cells, effector memory CD4+ T cells, and effector memory CD8+ T cells), the SC score that collects the expression related to immune-suppressive cells (T-regs and Myeloid-derived suppressor cells), the Major Histocompatibility Complex score for MHC-related molecules, and the immune checkpoint score to represent the activity of immune checkpoints or immunomodulators47.

In ductal breast carcinoma, the presence of a high rate of chromosomal copy number variations is associated with an increased immune response, as well as the presence of tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes and PD-L1 gene expression49–52.
